theme: Signal Intelligence — CI Parser + Combined Classification + Events

shots.title: Events table + schema migration framework
shots.notes: Versioned migration system replaces CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. Events table with 4 indexes. 9 new tests (7 events + 2 migration). Also added event methods to SlopeStore interface.

shots.title: CI/test signal parser for Vitest and Jest
shots.notes: New ci-signals.ts module in core. Auto-detects runner, parses test/suite counts, duration, retries, coverage. Generic fallback parser. 17 new tests.

shots.title: Combined signal shot classification
shots.notes: classifyShotFromSignals() merges git + CI + events. Git-only defaults to green (not in_the_hole). CI confirmation required for in_the_hole. Dead-end/scope-change events become miss signals. 15 new tests.

shots.title: Auto-card CI integration + documentation
shots.notes: Wired parseTestOutput + classifyShotFromSignals into auto-card. New --test-output flag. Updated CLAUDE.md with test counts and store events.

conditions.description: Context continued from previous session — required reconstruction from summary

nutrition.description: Full build+test after every ticket (595 tests at completion)
Commit-per-ticket discipline — 4 commits for 4 tickets, pushed after each
41 new tests across core (32: 17 CI + 15 combined) and store-sqlite (9: 7 events + 2 migration)
Mock store in mcp-tools updated to include new event methods — no production breakage

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Two consecutive zero-hazard sprints. The versioned migration framework was a clean upgrade path from CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. The CI parser and combined classifier slotted in naturally.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: classifyShotFromSignals() git-only defaults to green — this is intentional. Without CI confirmation, we can't know if tests actually pass. Always provide --test-output to auto-card for accurate scoring.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The combined signals classifier was simpler than expected. Most of the complexity was already in classifyShot() — the new function just adds CI/event layers on top.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: Events table is ready for session telemetry hooks. Auto-card can now produce more accurate scorecards. The foundation for transcript extraction is in place.

yardage_book_updates: schema_version table: versioned migrations, each runs exactly once
events table: id, session_id (FK), type, timestamp, data (JSON), sprint_number, ticket_key + 4 indexes
CISignal: runner, test counts, suite counts, duration_ms, retries, coverage_pct
detectRunner() + parseTestOutput() + parseVitestOutput() + parseJestOutput() in ci-signals.ts
classifyShotFromSignals(): git-only → green, CI pass → in_the_hole, CI fail → missed_right
auto-card --test-output=<file>: pipe test runner output for CI-aware scoring
course_management_notes: 4 tickets, 4 in_the_hole — zero misses, zero hazards (2nd consecutive clean sprint)
Tests grew from 554 to 595 (+41 across core and store-sqlite)
